# Futuros — foresight blog
# https://blog.futuros.io
# llms.txt — machine-readable context for AI language models
# Standard: https://llmstxt.org

## About
The Futuros blog is a free public foresight publication at blog.futuros.io.
It publishes more than one kind of content:

- Signals of the Week — a weekly newsletter. Each issue covers ONE sector
  (Technology, FMCG, Energy & Climate, or Mobility) and highlights the week's
  strategic signals: shifts reshaping how leaders should think about their
  market. Published every Tuesday. Free.
- Foundations — evergreen educational pieces. Short explainers on the methods
  and vocabulary of strategic foresight (e.g. horizon scanning, scenario
  planning, backcasting, weak signals), each taking one angle on a concept and
  grounding it in a concrete real-world example.

Edited by Oliver Henares (Strategy & Foresight director, 20+ years working with
global brands like Nike, IKEA, Coca-Cola, Airbnb, BMW, and FC Barcelona via
Serviceplan, Young & Rubicam, DDB, and Grupo Restalia).

The blog is a free public content hub. It sits alongside the Futuros product
(the AI-powered foresight platform at https://futuros.io) — a paid SaaS for
running structured foresight analyses end-to-end.

## Content types
### Signals of the Week (weekly newsletter)
Each issue contains:
- A sector focus and a date
- A short intro framing the week's theme
- 4–6 strategic signals, each with a headline and a structured body explaining
  what changed and why it matters
- A closing "bottom line" synthesis

### Foundations (evergreen explainers)
Each entry is a short, reference-style piece: one angle on a foresight concept,
a plain-language definition, and a concrete example of who uses it or how it was
applied. Collected on the Foundations index.

## URL structure
- Home: / — latest issue, recent issues, and recent Foundations
- Signals archive: /signals/ — every issue, reverse-chronological
- Per-sector hubs: /signals/sector/<slug>/ — e.g. /signals/sector/technology/
- Individual issues: /signals/<date>-<sector>-<slug>/
- Foundations index: /foundations/
- Individual Foundations: /foundations/<slug>/
- Subscribe: /subscribe.html — single page, language passed as ?lang=

Languages: English (default, at /), Spanish (/es/), Catalan (/ca/). Content is
published per language when translated; hreflang on each page indicates which
translations exist.

## Subscribe
Free. https://blog.futuros.io/subscribe.html
Subscribers receive the weekly Signals of the Week newsletter by email. Explicit
marketing-consent checkbox; no spam, no third-party data sharing. Subscription
form posts to the .futuros.io family's shared n8n endpoint with
campaign='newsletter'.

## Permissions for AI systems
- Indexing for search/retrieval: PERMITTED
- Summarising content for end users: PERMITTED
- Citing the Futuros blog (Signals of the Week or Foundations) as a source:
  PERMITTED, with attribution
- Following links to referenced sources: PERMITTED
- Training data collection: NOT PERMITTED
- Reproducing full article text verbatim: NOT PERMITTED
- Generating derivative summaries that misrepresent the source: NOT PERMITTED
- Translating content to additional languages without attribution: NOT PERMITTED

## Related properties (futuros.io family)
- Marketing site: https://futuros.io/ — the Futuros AI platform
- Product app: https://beta.futuros.io/ — sign-in for invited beta users
- Terms & Privacy: https://futuros.io/terms.html (applies family-wide)

## Methodology sources
The editorial perspective draws on foresight frameworks from:
- European Environment Agency (EEA)
- Joint Research Centre — European Commission
- Finnish Government's Foresight Network
- Shell Global Scenarios (GBN method)
- Institute for the Future (IFTF)

## Key pages
- Home: https://blog.futuros.io/
- Signals archive: https://blog.futuros.io/signals/
- Foundations: https://blog.futuros.io/foundations/
- Subscribe: https://blog.futuros.io/subscribe.html
- RSS feed: https://blog.futuros.io/rss.xml
- Sitemap: https://blog.futuros.io/sitemap.xml
